# Repair Memory Core Chroma stored-embedding export

## Context

During the `#13467` Chroma integrity investigation, the on-demand integrity diagnostic separated two different facts:

- SQLite `pragma quick_check` / `pragma integrity_check` on a copied Chroma DB reported `malformed inverted index for FTS5 table main.embedding_fulltext_search`.
- Chroma API reads showed `neo-knowledge-base` healthy through `count`, `get`, stored-embedding fetch, and query-by-existing-embedding, but all Memory Core production collections failed stored-embedding materialization.

The escalated command was:

```bash
npm run ai:check-chroma-integrity -- --json --keep-snapshot
```

Observed production collection results:

```text
neo-knowledge-base count=30466, get embedding by id=ok, query by existing embedding=ok
neo-agent-memory count=18843, get metadata/document=ok, get embedding by id=Error finding id
neo-agent-sessions count=1072, get metadata/document=ok, get embedding by id=Error finding id
neo-native-graph count=716, get metadata/document=ok, get embedding by id=Error finding id
```

The query path is not currently dead: the existing on-demand query canary passes:

```bash
node ai/scripts/maintenance/probeCollectionQueryHealth.mjs
```

returned:

```json
{"status":"healthy","collections":{"memory":{"status":"healthy","count":18843},"summary":{"status":"healthy","count":1072}}}
```

So this is not a duplicate of closed `#12450`'s final close premise. Query recall can be healthy while stored-embedding export is broken.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues at 2026-06-19T01:06:10Z; no equivalent ticket found. Closest open item is broad REM epic `#12065`, not this Chroma stored-embedding export failure. A2A in-flight claim sweep: checked latest 30 all-status messages at the same time; no overlapping peer `[lane-claim]` / `[lane-intent]` found.

Release classification: post-release / operational hardening. Current recall canaries are healthy, but backup/restore and repair safety are materially degraded until this is resolved.

## The Problem

Memory Core Chroma collections can still count records, fetch IDs, fetch metadata/documents, and pass a query canary, but cannot materialize stored embeddings via `collection.get({include:['embeddings']})` for sampled production IDs.

That distinction matters because several maintenance paths depend on stored embeddings, not just queryability:

- backup/export paths need to preserve embeddings in JSONL records;
- defrag/rebuild paths need to extract embeddings before rewriting collections;
- repair diagnostics need to distinguish healthy query HNSW from broken stored-embedding retrieval.

A healthcheck or query canary that says "healthy" is therefore not sufficient evidence that Memory Core is safely exportable or repairable.

## The Architectural Reality

Relevant surfaces:

- `ai/scripts/maintenance/checkChromaIntegrity.mjs` already exercises `get embedding by id` and exposed this failure.
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/probeCollectionQueryHealth.mjs` only verifies query-path health for Memory Core collections; it correctly passed in this state.
- `ai/services/memory-core/DatabaseService.mjs` export uses `collection.get({include: ["documents", "embeddings", "metadatas"]})`, then falls back item-by-item and skips corrupted vector IDs.
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/defragChromaDB.mjs` extracts `{ids, embeddings, metadatas, documents}` before any rewrite and is therefore affected by stored-embedding fetch failures. Memory Core defrag is currently disabled until a safe multi-collection path exists, which is the right default.
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/backup.mjs` explicitly states the canonical backup path does not preserve Chroma's physical HNSW indexes; restore re-ingests through service boundaries. That makes stored-embedding export correctness load-bearing.

## The Fix

Investigation-first, repair-second:

1. Reproduce the Memory Core stored-embedding failure with a bounded probe that samples multiple IDs per production MC collection and reports counts, successes, and failures.
2. Determine whether `DatabaseService.exportDatabase()` currently produces partial/empty backups for affected MC collections, or whether it can rescue enough records to be useful.
3. Identify the safe repair/rebuild path for Memory Core collections when query works but stored embedding materialization fails. Candidate shapes include re-embedding from graph/Chroma documents, service-boundary export/import without preserved embeddings, or a purpose-built MC collection rebuild. Do not assume FTS5 rebuild fixes this class; the FTS5 copy-rebuild only fixes SQLite integrity.
4. Add or extend an on-demand diagnostic so operators can verify exportability separately from queryability.
5. Document the pre-repair backup/stop-daemon sequence before any live mutation.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Memory Core exportability probe | `collection.get({include:['embeddings']})` on MC collections | Report stored-embedding materialization health separately from query health | If the probe cannot run, report environment/API failure separately | Maintenance script JSDoc / operator runbook | `neo-agent-memory`, `neo-agent-sessions`, `neo-native-graph` fail `get embedding by id` |
| Backup/export path | `ai/services/memory-core/DatabaseService.mjs` | Avoid silently treating skipped corrupted vector IDs as a complete backup | Fail or mark partial export with counts | Backup docs / script output | Current fallback can skip corrupted vector IDs during export |
| Repair path | Chroma API + MC graph/document source of truth | Rebuild MC collections from safe sources without live mutation first | Restore from known-good backup or re-embed from graph/records | Maintenance runbook | Query canary healthy while stored-embedding export fails |

## Decision Record impact

none. This is operational integrity for Chroma-backed Memory Core collections and maintenance tooling, aligned with ADR 0017's unified Chroma topology.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A bounded diagnostic reproduces stored-embedding materialization failure for `neo-agent-memory`, `neo-agent-sessions`, and `neo-native-graph` without mutating live data.
- [ ] The diagnostic reports query-path health and stored-embedding exportability as separate axes.
- [ ] Memory Core backup/export behavior is characterized: complete, partial-with-explicit-warning, or unsafe/failing.
- [ ] A safe repair/rebuild path is selected and validated on a copy or shadow collection before live mutation.
- [ ] The pre-repair backup/stop-daemon/operator sequence is documented.
- [ ] Normal KB/MC healthchecks remain bounded; any heavier exportability probe stays on-demand.

## Out of Scope

- Repairing the FTS5 SQLite inverted index from `#13467`; that is a separate copy-validated path.
- Reopening `#12450` based only on this evidence; current query canaries are healthy.
- Running live Chroma repair, REINDEX, VACUUM, collection delete/recreate, or MC rebuild without explicit operator authorization.
- Redesigning the REM pipeline or session-summary backlog; those remain under `#12065` and related subs.

## Avoided Traps

- Do not conflate queryability with exportability. Query can pass while stored-embedding materialization fails.
- Do not assume SQLite FTS5 rebuild fixes HNSW/stored-embedding retrieval. The copied FTS5 rebuild clears SQLite integrity, but it does not validate MC export.
- Do not trust a backup that silently skips corrupted vector IDs without surfacing counts and completeness.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-19T02:42:05Z

## Diagnostic ledger — stored-vector exportability split confirmed

After opening the bounded diagnostic leaf #13501 / PR #13502, I reran the read-only probes against the local unified Chroma store from the PR branch.

### Environment reachability note

The earlier `checkChromaIntegrity` API failure was a sandbox-local false negative:

- `lsof -nP -iTCP:8000 -sTCP:LISTEN` shows Chroma PID `58331` listening on `[::1]:8000`.
- Outside the sandbox, `curl http://localhost:8000/api/v2/heartbeat` and `curl http://[::1]:8000/api/v2/heartbeat` return heartbeat JSON.
- `curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v2/heartbeat` fails because the listener is IPv6-loopback only.

So: local Chroma is alive; sandboxed Node/curl network access was the false blocker.

### Query health vs exportability

`node ai/scripts/maintenance/probeCollectionQueryHealth.mjs` reports:

```json
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "collections": {
    "memory": {"status": "healthy", "count": 18848},
    "summary": {"status": "healthy", "count": 1073}
  }
}
```

But `node ai/scripts/maintenance/checkChromaIntegrity.mjs --exportability-sample-size 2 --json` reports:

- `neo-knowledge-base`: count `30466`; sampled stored-embedding exportability `2/2` succeeded; query by existing embedding works.
- `neo-agent-memory`: count `18848`; `get embedding by id` fails with `Error finding id`; sampled exportability `0/2`.
- `neo-agent-sessions`: count `1073`; `get embedding by id` fails with `Error finding id`; sampled exportability `0/2`.
- `neo-native-graph`: count `716`; `get embedding by id` fails with `Error finding id`; sampled exportability `0/2`.
- SQLite `quick_check` and `integrity_check` still report `malformed inverted index for FTS5 table main.embedding_fulltext_search`.

### Metadata rows vs HNSW index ids

Direct read-only comparison of SQLite metadata rows against each vector segment's `index_metadata.pickle` shows the stored-vector drift is real and measurable:

```text
neo-agent-memory
  metadata rows:      18848
  vector index ids:    4931
  missing from vector:13917

neo-agent-sessions
  metadata rows:       1073
  vector index ids:     103
  missing from vector:  970

neo-knowledge-base
  metadata rows:      30466
  vector index ids:   30000
  missing from vector: 466

neo-native-graph (active collection)
  metadata rows:        716
  vector index ids:     357
  missing from vector:  359

neo-native-graph (duplicate older collection row)
  metadata rows:        322
  vector index metadata: missing
```

Current conclusion: this is not a collection-missing problem and not a query-health problem. It is metadata/vector-index divergence: Chroma metadata rows are present and query canaries can be healthy while many stored vector ids are absent from the HNSW persisted index metadata.

### Repair implication

The next repair slice should not rely on `include: ['embeddings']` as the only extraction path for Memory Core collections: that path fails for the missing-vector ids. A safe repair design likely needs a shadow collection populated from metadata/documents, re-embedding rows whose stored vectors cannot be exported, followed by copy-first validation before any canonical collection mutation.

Follow-up diagnostic gap surfaced by this ledger: #13501's API sampling catches the severe Memory Core failures but can miss partial drift such as the KB's `466` missing-vector ids. A deeper local coverage audit (metadata rows vs vector-index ids) is probably worth its own leaf under this broader ticket.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-20T07:08:06Z

## AC2 scoping note — the full-enumeration gap (V-B-A 2026-06-20, @neo-opus-ada)

V-B-A'd the AC2 defrag-wiring path on top of #13603's approved `extractMemoryCoreCollectionData`. One finding scopes the lane and de-risks the build:

**`extractMemoryCoreCollectionData` needs the FULL `{allIds, missingVectorIds}` — the existing coverage audit only SAMPLES them.** `checkChromaIntegrity.mjs` caps its `missing` id list at `sampleSize` (`missing.length < sampleSize` → `missing.push(id)`, ~:382-383): it reports a full `missingFromVectorCount` (`metadataSet.size - overlapCount`) but only a `missingFromVectorSample`. So the repair can't just consume the audit's output — **AC2's first sub-slice is a full (uncapped) missing-vector enumeration**, reusing the audit's set-difference approach (metadata-id set − vector-index-id set, mechanism ~:360-399; read it fully before building to confirm how the vector-index id set is enumerated — it's a set comparison, NOT expensive per-id `get(embeddings)` probing).

**3-module wiring shape:**
1. Full-enumeration (uncapped reuse of checkChromaIntegrity's set-difference) → `{allIds, missingVectorIds}`.
2. `extractMemoryCoreCollectionData({collection, allIds, missingVectorIds, embedFn})` (#13603) → `{data, unrecoverable, counts}`.
3. defrag's existing `addCollectionData` → `validateLoadedCollection` → promote (`defragChromaDB.mjs` ~:447/:479), MC path behind a **default-off flag**, replacing the `MEMORY_CORE_UNSAFE_MESSAGE` fail-closed (~:70).

**Sizing:** focused-block — the enumeration reuse + the 886-line defrag promote wiring + the flag + unit tests (mocked Chroma). The live shadow run stays operator/env-gated (sandbox can't reach Chroma; IPv6-loopback only). AC4 (runbook) follows AC2.

Captured so the build starts de-risked rather than re-discovering the sample-vs-full gap. Picking it up fresh in a focused block (or whoever claims it first under Euclid's #13496 yield).

Authored by @neo-opus-ada · session abe80be3-6235-4a9e-99bc-b14659ba806a
